records.description: npm global install EEXIST on bin path — prior install (often from a local node_modules/.bin) left a stale symlink; remove it before reinstalling, do not use --force

stderr_regex: npm error code EEXIST|EEXIST: file already exists.*\.(local|npm-global|nvm)/.*bin/

false_paths: running with --force overwrites whichever symlink target was there, including links owned by an unrelated project under node_modules/.bin

fix_path.description: Inspect the conflicting path with ls -l / readlink -f. If it is a symlink into another project's node_modules/.bin (a leftover from a local install), rm it then re-run npm install -g. Only fall back to --force after confirming nothing depends on the existing link.

applies_when: npm install -g (or equivalent) where the target bin path already exists
target path lives under a user-writable bin dir like ~/.local/bin, ~/.npm-global/bin, or an nvm prefix
do_not_apply_when: EEXIST raised during package extraction inside node_modules (different root cause: cache or concurrent install)
the existing file is a real binary not managed by npm — investigate provenance first

evidence: observed 2026-04-27: codex 0.124 upgrade via `npm install -g @openai/codex@latest` failed with EEXIST on ~/.local/bin/codex; readlink showed it pointed into ~/.openclaw/acpx-home/node_modules/.bin/codex (leftover from a prior local install). `rm` + reinstall succeeded.

legacy_v3.stderr_pattern: npm error code EEXIST|EEXIST: file already exists
legacy_v3.description: npm global install EEXIST on bin path — remove stale symlink (often from a prior local install) before reinstalling; avoid --force
